# Kamino Summary

Kamino is expanding RWA infrastructure on Solana, surpassing $1B in market size. Key updates include: ONyc deposit cap increased to $75M with 10.25% yield; $CASH now live in Superstate Market; users can loop leverage up to 2.5× and borrow USDC/USDS against tokenized assets like USCC and FWDI.

tweets.fullText:
RWAs are expanding rapidly on Solana, with Kamino recently surpassing $1B in total RWA market size.

These markets require robust credit infrastructure to provide the liquidity and risk frameworks that allow real-world assets to integrate with onchain capital.

Kamino is building https://t.co/qm82k0RM39
tweets.aiAnalysis:
# Why This Tweet Underperformed

The tweet relies heavily on self-promotional messaging (mentioning Kamino twice) and abstract infrastructure claims that lack concrete emotional hooks or urgency, making it feel more like a standard corporate announcement than shareable insight. While the $1B metric provides credibility, the vague reference to "building" without specificity, combined with limited visual content and niche appeal to crypto infrastructure enthusiasts, constrained viral potential compared to posts with clearer problem statements or surprising takes.

# Performance Analysis

The tweet underperformed relative to its views (11.6K) due to **niche crypto/DeFi positioning without broader emotional appeal**—it targets only existing protocol users interested in specific yield farming mechanics rather than triggering FOMO or curiosity among general audiences. While it includes concrete metrics ($50M→$75M increase, 10.25% yield, 2.5× leverage), these are too specialized and lack context for non-DeFi natives, limiting shares and genuine engagement despite reasonable visibility.

tweets.aiAnalysis:

The format also lacks visual differentiation (no embedded image/video) and specificity about impact (no numbers on potential gains or adoption metrics), which are critical for breaking through noise in crypto communities where similar announcements are constant.
